Member-only story

Welcome to the Agentic Age

4 min read
Just now

--

We just crossed a line most of us haven’t fully clocked yet.
There’s a before-AI world and an after-AI world, and we’re standing right at the seam.
Some people are calling it the Agentic Age — the age of the AI agent. If that phrase doesn’t mean anything to you yet, it will soon. It’s becoming the conversation. Oprah’s launching a whole podcast about it — the good, the bad, the ugly, the “this is how it all ends” theories.
I don’t think it’s the apocalypse. But I do think it changes everything. The real question is what’s actually on the other side of that line.
The Fear Box
Right now there are a hundred different futures lined up in front of us, and one of the ugliest ones is built entirely on fear.
Picture it: countries close ranks, corporations lock AI down to protect their own turf, and politicians — under pressure from people terrified of losing their paychecks — legislate it into a corner. If fear wins, AI ends up as nothing more than a glorified autocomplete. It opens your browser tabs. Drafts your emails. Builds your spreadsheets. That’s the ceiling.
